# `extract_citations`

**Extract Citations**

- **Source:** `courtlistener/mcp/tools/extract_citations_tool.py`
- **Estimated definition size:** ~214 tokens (description ~63, input schema ~74; cl100k_base)
- **Parameters:** 2 (1 required)
- **Raw input schema:** [`extract_citations.inputs.json`](./extract_citations.inputs.json)

## Description

Extract and resolve legal citations from text using eyecite.

Runs locally with no API calls or rate limits. Handles all
citation types: full case citations, id., supra, short cites,
and statutes.

Use this tool to understand the citation structure of a document
before selectively verifying citations with analyze_citations.

## Annotations

| Annotation | Value |
|---|---|
| `title` | `"Extract Citations"` |
| `readOnlyHint` | `true` |
| `destructiveHint` | `false` |
| `openWorldHint` | `false` |

## Parameters

### `text`

string · **required**

Legal text to extract citations from.

### `resolve`

boolean · optional · default: `true`

Whether to resolve id./supra/short cites to their antecedents. Defaults to true.
